output.summary: Four changes to the Accordion block: (1) Rebuild the test page using DemoContainer wrappers (new test page style) with comprehensive coverage of all options, (2) Review and update accessibility requirements for the accordion pattern, (3) Fix hover border color so border stays charcoal when a closed accordion item is hovered (remove not-open:hover:border-accent), (4) Ensure all attribute options and variations are covered by test page variations.

fileChanges.description: Remove 'not-open:hover:border-accent' from the accordion item classes so the border stays charcoal on hover.
fileChanges.details: Current classes on the <details> element: 'accordion-item group border border-charcoal rounded-3xl transition-all open:bg-accent-lighter not-open:hover:bg-accent not-open:hover:border-accent'. Change to: 'accordion-item group border border-charcoal rounded-3xl transition-all open:bg-accent-lighter not-open:hover:bg-accent'. The key change is REMOVING 'not-open:hover:border-accent' so the border stays charcoal when hovering over a closed item. The background still changes to accent on hover (not-open:hover:bg-accent), maintaining visual feedback, but the border remains charcoal for consistent definition.

fileChanges.description: Match the PHP border styling change -- remove 'not-open:hover:border-accent' from the TSX cn() classes.
fileChanges.details: In the cn() call on the <details> element, change the line 'not-open:hover:bg-accent not-open:hover:border-accent' to 'not-open:hover:bg-accent'. This removes the hover border color change so the border stays charcoal on hover, matching the PHP change. TSX and PHP must stay visually in sync.

fileChanges.description: Rebuild the test page using DemoContainer wrappers with comprehensive variation coverage.
fileChanges.details: Delete the existing 'test-accordion-exclusive-mode-qa' page and create a new 'test-accordion' page using takt/demo-container blocks for each variation. The new page must cover: (1) Default variation - minimal content, (2) Default variation - full content with eyebrow/heading/description/buttons, (3) With-media variation - image, (4) With-media variation - video, (5) Exclusive mode enabled, (6) Exclusive mode + always one open, (7) Always one open + auto-open first (no openByDefault on any item), (8) Default behavior (no exclusive mode), (9) Long content edge case, (10) Empty optional fields, (11) Open by default items, (12) Multiple items to test scrolling. Each variation wrapped in takt/demo-container with descriptive title and description.

designDecisions.question: Should the hover border color change be removed entirely, or replaced with a different color?
designDecisions.recommendation: Remove 'not-open:hover:border-accent' entirely so the border stays as border-charcoal on hover. The accent background (not-open:hover:bg-accent) already provides strong visual hover feedback. The charcoal border maintains consistent definition and visual structure. This is cleaner than picking a different hover border color.
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested 'Have the border continue with the same color when the card is hovered'. The charcoal border is the default/resting state color.

designDecisions.question: Should the test page be renamed from 'test-accordion-exclusive-mode-qa' to 'test-accordion' since it will now cover all accordion features?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes, rename to 'test-accordion' (slug: test-accordion) for consistency with other block test pages (test-cards-carousel, test-gallery-carousel, etc.). The old page was narrowly scoped to exclusive mode QA. The new page comprehensively covers all accordion options.
designDecisions.evidence: Other block test pages use the pattern 'test-{block-name-kebab}' (test-cards-carousel, test-gallery-carousel, test-masonry-cards).

designDecisions.question: Should the test page use DemoContainer wrappers for each variation?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes. The 'new test page style' uses takt/demo-container blocks instead of raw wp:heading + wp:paragraph blocks to label test sections. DemoContainer provides a structured title, description, role='region', and aria-label for accessibility. All recently created test pages (test-cards-carousel, etc.) follow this pattern.
designDecisions.evidence: Cards Carousel test page uses takt/demo-container for every variation. The DemoContainer block exists specifically for this purpose (inserter: false, only used programmatically).

accessibilityUpdates.currentStatus: PASS - The accordion currently uses native <details>/<summary> elements, which provide built-in keyboard support (Enter/Space to toggle) and screen reader announcements without additional ARIA.
accessibilityUpdates.proposedChange: No change needed. The native HTML disclosure pattern is the recommended approach per the block-type documentation and WAI-ARIA guidelines. This is more robust than custom ARIA accordion patterns.

accessibilityUpdates.requirement: Keyboard navigation: Enter/Space to toggle accordion items
accessibilityUpdates.currentStatus: PASS - Native <summary> elements are focusable and respond to Enter/Space by default.
PASS - Each accordion item title uses <h3> which is appropriate since the accordion heading uses <h2> (via ThemeHeading headingSize=2).
accessibilityUpdates.proposedChange: No change needed. The heading hierarchy is correct: section heading (h2) > accordion item titles (h3).

accessibilityUpdates.currentStatus: NEEDS VERIFICATION - The <summary> element should have a visible focus indicator. The current implementation uses cursor-pointer but no explicit focus-visible styling on the summary element.
accessibilityUpdates.proposedChange: Verify that the theme's global focus-visible styles apply to <summary> elements. If not, add focus-visible outline styling to the summary. This is a verification task, not necessarily a code change.

accessibilityUpdates.currentStatus: PASS - The accordion uses transition-all on the <details> element. Tailwind 4 respects prefers-reduced-motion by default for transitions.
accessibilityUpdates.proposedChange: No change needed. The transition-all class is governed by the prefers-reduced-motion media query in the theme's CSS configuration.

accessibilityUpdates.requirement: aria-disabled for always-one-open items that cannot be collapsed
accessibilityUpdates.currentStatus: NOT IMPLEMENTED - When alwaysOneOpen is true and only one item is open, that item cannot be collapsed. The WAI-ARIA accordion pattern recommends aria-disabled='true' on panels that cannot be collapsed.
accessibilityUpdates.proposedChange: Consider adding aria-disabled='true' to the <summary> of the currently open item when it is the last open item and alwaysOneOpen is enabled. However, since native <details>/<summary> does not natively support aria-disabled for preventing toggle, and the JS re-opens the item after toggle (rather than preventing it), this is a MINOR enhancement. The current JS approach (allowing toggle then immediately re-opening) may cause a brief visual flash. An alternative is to use event.preventDefault() on the summary click when the item should not close, but this requires careful handling with native <details>. Recommend: Add a note-level observation for future improvement, not a blocking change.

accessibilityUpdates.currentStatus: NEEDS VERIFICATION - When not-open:hover:bg-accent is applied, the text (charcoal/dark) on the accent background color needs to meet WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text). The accent colors vary per theme color (neon-green, blue, green, yellow, orange, purple).
accessibilityUpdates.proposedChange: Verify contrast ratios for all accent colors against charcoal text. The accordion item title uses font-heading text-lg/text-xl which may qualify as large text (3:1 ratio). Most accent colors should pass since they are bright against dark charcoal text, but verify orange and purple variants.

interactiveTests.name: Accordion item opens on click
interactiveTests.expect: Content panel becomes visible, details element gains open attribute.

interactiveTests.name: Multiple items can be open without exclusive mode
interactiveTests.expect: Both first and second items are open simultaneously.

interactiveTests.name: Exclusive mode closes sibling when opening new item
interactiveTests.expect: Item 2 opens and Item 1 (which was openByDefault) closes automatically.

interactiveTests.name: Always-one-open prevents closing last item
interactiveTests.expect: Item A is the only open item and clicking its summary should not close it (it re-opens immediately).

interactiveTests.name: Hover state shows accent background with charcoal border
interactiveTests.expect: Closed item shows accent background on hover but border remains charcoal (not accent color).

qaChecklist: STYLING: Hover border stays charcoal on closed items (not-open:hover:border-accent removed)
STYLING: Hover background still changes to accent on closed items (not-open:hover:bg-accent preserved)
STYLING: Open items have accent-lighter background (open:bg-accent-lighter preserved)
STYLING: Open items have charcoal border (border-charcoal default, no override)
STYLING: TSX and PHP classes match exactly
BEHAVIOR: Exclusive mode - opening one item closes the previously open item
BEHAVIOR: Exclusive mode - can close the only open item (when alwaysOneOpen is false)
BEHAVIOR: Always-one-open - cannot close the last remaining open item
BEHAVIOR: Always-one-open + no openByDefault - first item auto-opens on page load
BEHAVIOR: Default mode - multiple items can be open simultaneously
BEHAVIOR: openByDefault attribute correctly opens items on initial render
LAYOUT: Default variation two-column grid renders correctly
LAYOUT: With-media variation with image renders correctly
LAYOUT: Long content wraps without breaking layout
LAYOUT: Many items (8+) maintain consistent spacing
A11Y: Native <details>/<summary> provides keyboard support (Enter/Space)
A11Y: Focus indicator visible on summary elements
A11Y: Heading hierarchy is correct (h2 section heading > h3 item titles)
A11Y: Contrast ratio passes on hover state (accent bg + charcoal text)
TEST PAGE: All variations wrapped in DemoContainer with title and description
TEST PAGE: Page slug is 'test-accordion' following naming convention
